description.en: The evaluated value of the interpolation. >>> t'{1 + 2}'.interpolations[0].value 3

excerpt.es: The evaluated value of the interpolation. >>> t'{1 + 2}'.interpolations[0].value 3
excerpt.en: The evaluated value of the interpolation. >>> t'{1 + 2}'.interpolations[0].value 3

description.en: A tuple of all interpolated values in the template. >>> cheese = 'Camembert' >>> template = t'Ah! We do have {cheese}.' >>> template.values ('Camembert',) The values tuple always has the same length as the interpolations tuple. It is always equivalent to tuple(i.value for i in template.interpolations).
